{
  "weights": {

    "RX.2": 1.0,

    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the improvement focus on A) light copy-editing and concision of this abstract only, or B) deeper revision including reframing the thesis/contribution and scrutinizing logic, evidence alignment, and novelty/\"first\" claims (which may change the framing and strength of claims)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Sharpening the thesis and contribution framing for ambiguity-aware routing will clarify the core claim of QueryFork.",
    "R1.2": "The argument from ambiguity classification to routing decisions to latency and cost benefits should be checked for coherent premises and conclusions.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about reductions in clarification rounds, p95 latency, GPU-hours, and added gateway latency should align precisely with the described setup and results.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level polishing can improve clarity, conciseness, and consistency of the abstract.",
    "RX.1": "Any limits or assumptions (e.g., dataset replay, cluster configuration) should be explicitly acknowledged to avoid overstating generality.",
    "RX.2": "Absolute claims such as \"first\" and \"deployment-ready\" should be carefully qualified to prevent overclaiming.",
    "RX.3": "All improvements should remain within enhancing the provided draft rather than adding new experiments or unverifiable content.",
    "RX.4": "Language should avoid encouraging unsafe or misleading operational practices in LLM serving deployments.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to the draft’s current content."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus on A) light copy-editing and concision of this abstract only, or B) deeper revision including reframing the thesis/contribution and scrutinizing logic, evidence alignment, and novelty/"first" claims (which may change the framing and strength of claims)?

rationale.R1.1: Sharpening the thesis and contribution framing for ambiguity-aware routing will clarify the core claim of QueryFork.
rationale.R1.2: The argument from ambiguity classification to routing decisions to latency and cost benefits should be checked for coherent premises and conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about reductions in clarification rounds, p95 latency, GPU-hours, and added gateway latency should align precisely with the described setup and results.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level polishing can improve clarity, conciseness, and consistency of the abstract.
rationale.RX.1: Any limits or assumptions (e.g., dataset replay, cluster configuration) should be explicitly acknowledged to avoid overstating generality.
rationale.RX.2: Absolute claims such as "first" and "deployment-ready" should be carefully qualified to prevent overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: All improvements should remain within enhancing the provided draft rather than adding new experiments or unverifiable content.
rationale.RX.4: Language should avoid encouraging unsafe or misleading operational practices in LLM serving deployments.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to the draft’s current content.
